Reaction: Cholesterol → 25-HC
TriSI_Relevance: ISG; induced by type I IFN and TLR3/4. Direct bridge from cGAS-STING/ISG module to oxysterol production.
Chicken_Annotation_Risk: MEDIUM — intronless gene; verify Ensembl ID in GRCg7b. May appear as LOC identifier.
Key_References: Liu et al. 2013 Immunity; Blanc et al. 2013 Immunity; Reboldi et al. 2014 Science

Reaction: 25-HC → 7α,25-diHC (GPR183 ligand)
TriSI_Relevance: Produces the immune cell chemoattractant. LPS-inducible in macrophages. Shapes oxysterol gradient for OCP recruitment.
Chicken_Annotation_Risk: LOW — CYP450 family well-annotated in chicken
Key_References: Hannedouche et al. 2011 Nature; Yi et al. 2012 Immunity

Reaction: Cholesterol → 27-OHC
TriSI_Relevance: Macrophage-derived 27-OHC promotes osteoclastogenesis via LXR/RANKL. Mitochondrial P450.
Key_References: Nelson et al. 2011 Endocrinology; DuSell et al. 2010

Reaction: Cholesterol → 24(S)-OHC
TriSI_Relevance: Primarily neural; included for metabolomic completeness. Less direct bone relevance.
Chicken_Annotation_Risk: MEDIUM — may be absent or poorly annotated in non-neural chicken tissues
Key_References: Russell 2003 Annu Rev Biochem

Reaction: Cholesterol → 7α-OHC (bile acid pathway)
TriSI_Relevance: Primarily hepatic rate-limiting bile acid enzyme. Low priority for disc tissue but included for metabolomic panel context.
Key_References: Russell 2003 Annu Rev Biochem

Reaction: Cholesterol → pregnenolone; also produces 20(S)-OHC and 22(R)-OHC as intermediates
TriSI_Relevance: Source of endogenous osteogenic oxysterols 20(S)-OHC and 22(R)-OHC. Mitochondrial.
Key_References: Dwyer et al. 2007 J Biol Chem

Reaction: Oxysterol receptor (allosteric activation bypassing PTCH1)
TriSI_Relevance: Oxysterols (20S-OHC, Oxy133) directly bind SMO to activate Hh signaling and osteoblast differentiation. Cyclopamine-blockable.
Key_References: Dwyer et al. 2007 J Biol Chem; Montgomery et al. 2012 J Bone Miner Res; Nachtergaele et al. 2012 Nat Chem Biol

Reaction: Hh receptor; constitutive SMO repressor. Oxysterols may bypass PTCH1 repression.
TriSI_Relevance: Core Hh pathway; also a Hh target gene (feedback). SHH overexpression in NP blocked sacral fusion (Bonavita et al. 2018).
Key_References: Bonavita et al. 2018 Biol Open (cited in PNAS 2023)

Reaction: Hh transcriptional activator; direct readout of pathway activation
TriSI_Relevance: GLI-reporter activation by oxysterols demonstrated. Downstream effector of SMO.
Key_References: Montgomery et al. 2012 J Bone Miner Res

Reaction: Hh TF; bifunctional activator/repressor
TriSI_Relevance: Cooperates with GLI1 in osteogenic program.
Key_References: Hui & Angers 2011 Annu Rev Cell Dev Biol

Reaction: Primarily Hh repressor (GLI3R); processed to repressor in absence of Hh
TriSI_Relevance: Repressor form inhibits osteogenic targets; pathway activation converts to activator.
Key_References: Hui & Angers 2011

Reaction: Notch target gene; induced by oxysterols via non-canonical (SMO-dependent) Notch signaling
TriSI_Relevance: siRNA knockdown inhibits oxysterol-induced osteogenic genes. Part of oxysterol → SMO → Notch crosstalk.
Key_References: Kim et al. 2010 J Bone Miner Res

Full_Name: Hes related family bHLH TF with YRPW motif 1
Reaction: Notch target; induced by oxysterol/SMO signaling; partially LXR-dependent
TriSI_Relevance: siRNA knockdown inhibits oxysterol-induced osteogenesis. LXR knockdown partially blocks HEY1 induction.
Key_References: Kim et al. 2010 J Bone Miner Res

Reaction: Oxysterol receptor for 7α,25-diHC; chemotactic GPCR on immune cells and OC precursors
TriSI_Relevance: Directs monocyte/OCP migration to bone surfaces along oxysterol gradients. OBs secrete ligand. Critical for coupled remodeling.
Chicken_Annotation_Risk: MEDIUM — GPCR; verify annotation in GRCg7b. May be annotated as EBI2.
Key_References: Hannedouche et al. 2011 Nature; Nevius et al. 2015 J Exp Med

Reaction: Degrades 7α,25-diHC → inactive; shapes chemotactic gradient
TriSI_Relevance: Gradient-sculpting enzyme. High expression = low ligand zone. Important for spatial patterning of immune recruitment.
Key_References: Yi et al. 2012 Immunity

Reaction: Nuclear receptor activated by oxysterols; promotes cholesterol efflux; anti-inflammatory via NF-κB transrepression; but also induces RANKL in osteoblasts
TriSI_Relevance: Dual role: anti-inflammatory (NF-κB suppression) AND pro-resorptive (27-OHC → LXR → RANKL). Key integration node.
Key_References: Nelson et al. 2011 Endocrinology; Tall & Yvan-Charvet 2015 Nat Rev Immunol

Reaction: Ubiquitous LXR isoform; similar oxysterol responsiveness
TriSI_Relevance: Broadly expressed complement to LXRα.
Key_References: Tall & Yvan-Charvet 2015 Nat Rev Immunol

Reaction: LXR target; cholesterol efflux to ApoA-I. Readout of LXR activation.
TriSI_Relevance: Cholesterol efflux suppresses TLR signaling and inflammasome (membrane raft disruption). Key LXR effector.
Key_References: Tall & Yvan-Charvet 2015 Nat Rev Immunol

Reaction: LXR target; cholesterol efflux to HDL
TriSI_Relevance: Cooperates with ABCA1 for cholesterol efflux. Efflux deficiency amplifies inflammasome.
Key_References: Tall & Yvan-Charvet 2015

Reaction: Master TF for cholesterol biosynthesis; processing suppressed by oxysterols via INSIG/SCAP
TriSI_Relevance: SCAP-SREBF2 complex physically associates with NLRP3 for inflammasome assembly (Guo et al. 2018 Immunity). Bridge between cholesterol sensing and inflammasome.
Key_References: Guo et al. 2018 Immunity; Reboldi et al. 2014 Science

Reaction: ER cholesterol sensor; escorts SREBP2 to Golgi when cholesterol is low. NLRP3 scaffolding partner.
TriSI_Relevance: SCAP-SREBP2-NLRP3 ternary complex required for optimal inflammasome assembly. Statin treatment (cholesterol depletion) amplifies inflammasome via this axis.
Predicted_Direction_Fusing: UNCERTAIN (scaffolding role; transcript may not change)
Key_References: Guo et al. 2018 Immunity

Reaction: Rate-limiting enzyme of cholesterol biosynthesis; SREBP2 target
TriSI_Relevance: Oxysterols suppress via INSIG/SCAP/SREBP2. Readout of cholesterol biosynthetic flux.
Predicted_Direction_Fusing: DOWN (suppressed by oxysterol-INSIG axis) or UP (if cholesterol demand high)
Key_References: Brown & Goldstein 1997 Cell

Reaction: ER retention of SCAP-SREBP2 when bound to oxysterols; negative feedback on cholesterol biosynthesis
TriSI_Relevance: Direct oxysterol sensor. Oxysterol binding → INSIG-SCAP interaction → SREBP2 retained in ER.
Predicted_Direction_Fusing: UP (induced by SREBP2 as negative feedback)
Key_References: Radhakrishnan et al. 2007 Cell Metab

Reaction: Similar ER retention function as INSIG1; constitutively expressed
TriSI_Relevance: Complementary oxysterol sensor.
Key_References: Radhakrishnan et al. 2007

Reaction: Cholesterol uptake receptor; SREBP2 target
TriSI_Relevance: Readout of SREBP2 transcriptional activity.
Key_References: Brown & Goldstein 1997

Reaction: Upstream of HMGCR in mevalonate pathway; SREBP2 target
TriSI_Relevance: Cholesterol biosynthesis marker; co-regulated with HMGCR.
Key_References: Guo et al. 2018 Immunity

Reaction: Sulfonates oxysterols (7-KC, 25-HC, etc.) → inactive sulfated forms
TriSI_Relevance: Major oxysterol inactivation pathway. Low expression = prolonged oxysterol signaling.
Predicted_Direction_Fusing: UNCERTAIN (low expression would amplify oxysterol effects)
Chicken_Annotation_Risk: MEDIUM — sulfotransferase family; verify in GRCg7b
Key_References: Fuda et al. 2007 J Lipid Res

Reaction: Interconverts 7-KC ↔ 7β-OHC. Hepatic detoxification; also cortisol/cortisone conversion.
TriSI_Relevance: Metabolizes toxic 7-KC. Also relevant to glucocorticoid biology (prednisolone experiments).
Key_References: Anderson et al. 2020 Redox Biol

Reaction: Lysosomal cholesterol/oxysterol export to ER and PM
TriSI_Relevance: NPC1 disruption blocks NLRP3 inflammasome activation by trapping cholesterol in lysosomes (de la Roche et al. 2018 J Cell Biol).
Key_References: de la Roche et al. 2018 J Cell Biol

Reaction: Lysosomal cholesterol transfer protein; works with NPC1
TriSI_Relevance: Cooperative with NPC1 for cholesterol egress.
Key_References: Infante et al. 2008 PNAS

Reaction: Intracellular oxysterol transport at ER-Golgi membrane contact sites
TriSI_Relevance: Intracellular oxysterol distribution; may influence compartmentalized signaling.
Key_References: Mesmin et al. 2017 Curr Opin Cell Biol

Reaction: Cholesterol transport to mitochondrial inner membrane (for CYP11A1 and CYP27A1)
TriSI_Relevance: Rate-limiting step for mitochondrial oxysterol production. Induced by cAMP/PKA.
Key_References: Stocco 2001 Annu Rev Physiol

Reaction: Non-vesicular cholesterol transport between ER and PM
TriSI_Relevance: Intracellular cholesterol distribution; SREBP2 target.
Key_References: Rodriguez-Agudo et al. 2011 Biochim Biophys Acta

Reaction: Master osteoblast TF; induced by oxysterol-SMO-Hh cascade; DNA binding enhanced by oxysterol via PKC
TriSI_Relevance: Already in your osteoblast panel. Include here as convergence point from oxysterol Arm A.
Key_References: Kha et al. 2004 J Bone Miner Res; Richardson et al. 2007 J Cell Biochem

Reaction: Osteoblast commitment TF downstream of RUNX2; induced by oxysterols
TriSI_Relevance: Already in osteoblast panel. Oxysterol-inducible.
Key_References: Montgomery et al. 2012 J Bone Miner Res

Reaction: Osteoblast activity marker; induced by oxysterols
TriSI_Relevance: Already in osteoblast panel. Key functional readout of osteogenic differentiation.
Key_References: Kha et al. 2004; Aghaloo et al. 2007 J Orthop Res

Reaction: Mature osteoblast marker; induced by oxysterols
TriSI_Relevance: Already in osteoblast panel. Late-stage osteogenic marker.
Key_References: Kha et al. 2004; Montgomery et al. 2012
